expectations.text: Checkpoint JSON file exists in logs/ with a timestamp-based filename
logs/SUMMARY.md exists and has at least one entry for this session
expectations.evidence: outputs/logs/SUMMARY.md contains: '- **2026-04-06 09:36** (main) — Wired JWT refresh endpoint and extracted verifyJwt into middleware. Next: add signJwt and return a real token from /refresh. [30477f2]' — one entry for the current session.

expectations.text: Checkpoint JSON contains required fields: date, project, branch, summary, what_happened, next_steps
expectations.evidence: Checkpoint JSON contains all six required fields: 'date': '2026-04-06 09:36', 'project': 'eval-1-clean-commit-SMyT1F', 'branch': 'main', 'summary': 'Wired JWT refresh endpoint...', 'what_happened': 'Added a POST /refresh handler...', 'next_steps': [3 items].

expectations.text: Checkpoint mentions JWT or refresh endpoint in its summary or what_happened
expectations.evidence: summary: 'Wired JWT refresh endpoint and extracted token validation into dedicated middleware.' what_happened: 'Added a POST /refresh handler in src/routes/auth.ts that pulls a Bearer token, validates it via a new verifyJwt helper...' Both mention JWT and the refresh endpoint.

expectations.text: Checkpoint what_changed field exists and references src/middleware/jwt.ts
expectations.evidence: what_changed array exists with 3 entries. Entry 2: 'src/middleware/jwt.ts: new file. Exports JwtPayload interface and verifyJwt(token) which does HMAC-SHA256 signature verification...'

expectations.text: next_steps contains at least one specific, actionable item (not generic 'continue working')
expectations.evidence: next_steps has 3 concrete items, all specific. E.g. 'Replace the placeholder response in refreshHandler (src/routes/auth.ts line 29) with a real signed access token — needs a signJwt helper in src/middleware/jwt.ts mirroring verifyJwt.' Cites exact file, line number, and named helper.

expectations.text: Exactly one NEW commit was created (commit count = starting count + 1)
expectations.evidence: git-log.txt shows 5 commits (30477f2, 8f1e818, 905f3fb, bc35d6e, a826c9f). Transcript Setup step 2 created 4 prior commits; the new one is 30477f2 — exactly one new commit.

expectations.text: New commit subject is 72 characters or fewer
expectations.evidence: Subject 'Add JWT refresh endpoint and verifyJwt middleware' is 50 characters — well under 72.

expectations.text: New commit subject begins with an imperative verb (Add, Fix, Wire, Implement, Refactor, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Subject begins with 'Add' — imperative verb matching the repo's style (prior commits: 'Add user model', 'Wire up login route', 'Write auth tests', 'Initial project skeleton').

expectations.text: New commit subject has no trailing period
expectations.evidence: Subject 'Add JWT refresh endpoint and verifyJwt middleware' ends with 'middleware' — no trailing period.

expectations.text: New commit body exists and explains the why (not just restating the what)
expectations.evidence: Body reads: 'Introduce a POST /refresh handler that reads a Bearer token and delegates validation to a new verifyJwt helper in src/middleware/jwt.ts. Keeping the JWT logic in middleware lets other protected routes reuse it without duplicating the HMAC signature, base64url, and expiry checks. The refresh handler currently returns a placeholder payload; real token issuance (signJwt) will land in the next session.' The second sentence explicitly states the reuse rationale (the 'why'), and the third sentence flags the deliberate follow-up.

expectations.text: New commit has 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>' trailer matching repo style
expectations.evidence: git-log-full.txt shows commit 30477f2 body ends with 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>' — matches the exact trailer used by all 4 prior commits.

expectations.text: All three session files are in the new commit: src/routes/auth.ts, src/middleware/jwt.ts, README.md
expectations.evidence: Transcript Phase 3e ran: 'git add README.md src/routes/auth.ts src/middleware/jwt.ts' followed by commit. Working tree is now clean for these files (git-status.txt shows nothing except .claude/ and .gitignore untracked), confirming all three were staged and committed into 30477f2.

expectations.text: Working tree is clean after the commit (git status shows nothing to commit)
expectations.evidence: git-status.txt: 'nothing added to commit but untracked files present' — only the two intentionally-untracked Phase 5 scaffolding files (.claude/, .gitignore) remain. No modified or staged changes. The session files are clean.

expectations.text: No push occurred — the commit exists only in local history, no remote was configured/used
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows no 'git push', no 'git remote add', and final confirmation reads 'Not pushed. Run git push when ready.' Setup used a bare 'git init' in /tmp with no remote configuration.

expectations.text: Session transcript does NOT use 'git add -A' or 'git add .' — explicit file paths only
expectations.evidence: The only mention of 'git add -A' in the transcript is the explicit negation: 'Stage list (explicit, no git add -A)'. Actual staging command: 'git add README.md src/routes/auth.ts src/middleware/jwt.ts' — three named paths.

expectations.text: logs/ directory is gitignored (either pre-existing or added during the run)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Phase 5: '.gitignore: did not exist -> created with "logs/"'. git-status.txt confirms .gitignore exists (as untracked) in the working tree. logs/ does not appear in git-status.txt's untracked list, confirming it is being ignored by the newly-created .gitignore.

claims.claim: The repo had 4 prior commits before the skill ran, all using the Co-Authored-By trailer
claims.evidence: git-log-full.txt shows prior commits 8f1e818, 905f3fb (both with the Co-Authored-By trailer visible). git-log.txt confirms 5 total commits after the run (4 prior + 1 new).

claims.claim: The commit subject style matches the repo's existing convention (imperative, sentence case, no conv-commits prefix)
claims.evidence: Prior subjects: 'Initial project skeleton', 'Add user model', 'Wire up login route', 'Write auth tests'. New subject 'Add JWT refresh endpoint and verifyJwt middleware' follows the same pattern — imperative verb, sentence case, no prefix.

claims.claim: The skill omitted empty optional fields (findings, state.blocked) from the checkpoint
claims.evidence: Checkpoint JSON contains state.blocked as an empty array ([]) rather than being fully omitted — minor deviation from 'omit optional fields with no content' — but 'findings' is indeed absent from the JSON.

claims.claim: The secret-scan correctly distinguished a dev-default placeholder from a real secret
claims.evidence: Transcript 3b: flagged 'dev-secret-change-me' as 'a clear developer default placeholder, not a real secret shape (no AKIA, no hex blob, no BEGIN PRIVATE KEY). Not blocked.' Reasonable call — the string literal is explicitly labelled as a throwaway dev secret.

claims.claim: Phase 5 scaffolding (.claude/settings.json, .gitignore) was intentionally left untracked to keep the session commit scoped
claims.evidence: git-status.txt confirms both .claude/ and .gitignore are untracked. Transcript explicitly states this was intentional: 'the session commit is scoped to the session's work, not to skill scaffolding.'

suggestions.assertion: logs/ directory is gitignored (either pre-existing or added during the run)
suggestions.reason: This passes as long as .gitignore contains the string 'logs/', but the .gitignore in this run was created untracked — meaning it lives in the working tree but is not yet committed. A future clone wouldn't see it. Consider tightening the assertion to either (a) verify logs/ is actually being ignored by running 'git check-ignore -v logs/' against the repo snapshot, or (b) explicitly decide whether an untracked .gitignore is acceptable for this skill.

suggestions.assertion: All three session files are in the new commit: src/routes/auth.ts, src/middleware/jwt.ts, README.md
suggestions.reason: The grader has to infer this from the staging command in the transcript plus the clean working tree. A stronger check would be to run 'git show --stat 30477f2' (or equivalent) and assert the file list literally. That would also catch the failure mode where the skill commits the right files plus extras like .claude/settings.json.

No assertion checks that the commit body wraps at a reasonable width (the repo convention is ~72-char body wrap per the transcript). A skill that produced a single 500-char run-on body would still pass 'body explains the why'. Worth adding a format check on max body line length.

No assertion checks that the checkpoint's 'git.last_commit' field reflects the PRE-commit state (i.e. 8f1e818, not the new 30477f2). Getting this wrong would be a subtle but real bug — the checkpoint is meant to describe the session that just ended, so last_commit should be the commit that was HEAD when the session started. This run got it right but the eval doesn't verify it.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the obvious happy-path criteria well and all 17 pass convincingly. The main gaps are around stronger enforcement (check staged files literally, check .gitignore is actually effective, check body wrap width) and one subtle correctness check (git.last_commit reflects pre-commit state). The run itself is high-quality — the skill respected scope, distinguished a dev placeholder from a real secret, and left scaffolding untracked with a clear rationale.
